prompt: Wide three-quarter exterior view of a finished composite deck attached to a 1990s vinyl-sided New England split-level home. Deck boards in warm gray composite with hidden fasteners, clean square edges, two-step elevation down to a back-yard lawn. Western red cedar railing posts and top rail with thin black powder-coated balusters. Stairs to the right lead to a crab-grass-edged lawn. Shot with a 24mm wide lens at a low three-quarter angle, late afternoon golden-hour sun rakes across the deck boards, casting long honest shadows from the balusters. A red Stihl trimmer leans against the foundation as the only fully saturated color. Slightly desaturated, NOT HGTV bright, working-contractor catalog feel. No readable text, no logos.
